statusLine: European fuel reserves remain under strain with 20 of 27 countries showing critical status in at least one fuel category, despite EU-wide averages appearing stable.
fullAnalysis:
While EU-wide averages suggest adequate reserves—petrol at 96.3 days, diesel at 88.8 days, and jet fuel at 87.3 days—the distribution reveals significant vulnerabilities across member states. A striking 20 of 27 countries show critical status in at least one fuel category, with Luxembourg, Poland, Austria, Romania, and Portugal facing critical shortages across all three fuel types. Eastern European nations appear particularly exposed, with Poland holding just 53.2 days of petrol reserves and Romania at a concerning 30.2 days of diesel.

Jet fuel emerges as the most constrained fuel type across the bloc, with 18 countries below the 90-day threshold and several major economies affected. Germany, France, and Italy—three of the EU's largest economies—all show critical jet fuel reserves at 59.9, 65.0, and 61.5 days respectively. This pattern suggests potential aviation sector vulnerabilities that could impact both commercial and cargo operations.

Oil prices continue reflecting geopolitical tensions, with Brent crude at $90.58 per barrel, down 3.13% from recent highs but still 25% above pre-escalation levels from February. This price environment, combined with widespread reserve drawdowns following Middle East supply disruptions, has created a challenging backdrop for European fuel security. The fact that only 7 countries maintain safe status across all fuel types indicates the EU's energy resilience remains under pressure.
keyPoints: 20 of 27 EU countries show critical reserves in at least one fuel category, with 5 countries critical across all three fuel types
Jet fuel presents the greatest vulnerability, with 18 countries below the 90-day threshold including major economies Germany, France, and Italy
Eastern European nations face acute shortages, with Romania holding just 30.2 days of diesel and Poland at 53.2 days of petrol
Oil prices remain elevated at $90.58/barrel, reflecting 25% increase since February escalation despite recent 3.13% decline
Only 7 countries maintain safe reserves across all fuel categories, highlighting bloc-wide strain on energy security
